name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development and multi-technique materials characterization. Expertise in small molecule, polymer, and nanoparticle analysis using HPLC/UPLC, LC-MS, GC-MS, spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR), thermal analysis (TGA/DSC), and rheology. Current postdoctoral research focuses on biopolymer nanoparticle process development — directly aligned with drug delivery system characterization. Strong foundation in DOE, statistical analysis, and analytical method development from first principles.

education.details: Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science

experience.role: Postdoctoral Researcher — Biopolymer Nanoparticle Process Development
bullets: Lead comprehensive analytical characterization of biopolymer nanoparticles using HPLC, GPC/SEC, particle sizing (DLS), spectroscopy (FT-IR, UV-Vis), thermal analysis (TGA/DSC), and rheology.
Develop and optimize nanoparticle formulation processes using DOE, correlating process parameters with critical quality attributes (size, PDI, drug loading, release kinetics).
Apply HRMS and LC-MS techniques for molecular weight confirmation and impurity profiling of polymeric drug delivery systems.
Collaborate cross-functionally with synthesis and formulation teams to establish structure-property relationships guiding material selection and process development.

experience.role: Graduate Researcher — Analytical Chemistry & Materials Characterization
bullets: Developed and validated analytical methods for small molecule characterization using HPLC, GC-MS, NMR, and spectroscopic techniques.
Conducted 2D-LC/MS and GC/MS analysis for complex mixture separation and impurity identification in organic materials.
Performed comprehensive thermal and spectroscopic characterization to establish degradation pathways and stability profiles.
Authored technical reports and presented findings at cross-functional team meetings and scientific conferences.

skills.Thermal & Physical: TGA, DSC, rheology, DLS, particle sizing
skills.Data Analysis: DOE, SPC, multivariate analysis, Origin, JMP, MestReNova, ChemDraw
skills.Techniques: Method development, validation, transfer, stability studies
